title: Efficient Language-Guided Radiance Field Reconstruction for 3D Language Field Modeling
problem: Current methods for radiance field reconstruction struggle to incorporate open-vocabulary language inputs efficiently, especially in complex scenes with high visual fidelity In particular, scalable 3D language field modeling remains difficult when queries must stay open-vocabulary and mask-free.
existing_methods: Methods like Gaussian Splatting [2022] and Mip-NeRF360 [Barron et al.] provide high-quality radiance field rendering but lack efficient integration with language inputs. Lerf [CLIP embeddings] supports open-vocabulary queries but requires region proposals or masks, limiting scalability.
motivation: The ability to reconstruct high-fidelity 3D scenes from natural language descriptions is critical for applications such as virtual reality, augmented reality, and semantic scene understanding This matters because 3D language field modeling should support efficient open-vocabulary interaction in real scenes.
hypothesis: Integrating CLIP embeddings directly into Gaussian Splatting frameworks can enable efficient and high-quality radiance field reconstruction guided by open-vocabulary language inputs without requiring explicit region proposals or masks within a 3D language field.
method: Propose a method that embeds natural language descriptions into CLIP embeddings and fuses them with Gaussian Splatting during radiance field reconstruction. This approach avoids the need for region proposals or masks by leveraging hierarchical CLIP embeddings to guide the placement and density of Gaussian points in the 3D space The resulting representation is optimized specifically as a 3D language field rather than a generic text-guided scene generator.
evaluation: Evaluate on benchmark datasets using quantitative metrics such as PSNR and SSIM, alongside qualitative assessments of language-guided scene generation. Compare against existing methods like Lerf and Gaussian Splatting to demonstrate improvements in efficiency and visual quality.
significance: This work bridges the gap between language and 3D radiance fields, enabling more intuitive and scalable scene reconstruction from natural language inputs This would strengthen efficient 3D language field modeling for open-vocabulary interaction.
caveats: The method assumes access to high-quality CLIP embeddings and may face challenges in scenes with ambiguous or sparse language descriptions.
